properties.name: Initial surface search, South China Sea (east of Malay Peninsula)
properties.operator: Malaysia (KL ARCC), with aircraft/vessels from China, Japan, Singapore, Thailand, US, Vietnam
properties.outcome: No MH370 debris found; suspended 15 March 2014 when SATCOM analysis showed the flight continued for hours after last radar contact
properties.source: Dates, operator and 573,000 km2 fetched from ATSB final report Table 6; polygon approximated from figure description (waters between Malaysia and Vietnam around the last secondary-radar position near waypoint IGARI, 6.94 N 103.59 E)

properties.name: Initial surface search, Strait of Malacca / Andaman Sea (west of Malay Peninsula)
properties.operator: Malaysia (Royal Malaysian Air Force), with assets from 12 nations
properties.outcome: No debris found; based on primary radar track of an unidentified aircraft (thought to be MH370) flying up the Strait of Malacca
properties.source: Dates, operator and 4,560,000 km2 fetched from ATSB final report Table 7 (Strait of Malacca, Andaman Sea, Bay of Bengal and west of Sumatra); polygon is a coarse approximation covering the strait and Andaman Sea core of that region and is much smaller than the full reported 4.56M km2 envelope

properties.name: Southern Indian Ocean surface search, initial area SW of Perth (S1/S2 and satellite-imagery zone)
properties.operator: AMSA (Australia), aircraft/vessels from Australia, NZ, US, Japan, China, South Korea
properties.outcome: Multiple satellite and aerial object sightings investigated (incl. objects 43-45 S, 90-97 E); none confirmed as MH370 debris
properties.source: Backed by fetched coordinates: ATSB final report gives the satellite-imagery search region as approximately 42 S - 46 S with sightings at 43-45 S / 90-97 E, initial 600,000 km2 zone ~2,500 km SW of Perth (Tables 9-12); polygon is that latitude/longitude box

properties.name: Southern Indian Ocean surface search, refined area (moved 1,100 km NE; drifted areas A/B)
properties.operator: AMSA / ATSB (Australia), multinational aircraft and vessels
properties.outcome: Over 50 objects sighted on 28 March alone; recovered items were fishing equipment and other flotsam, none from MH370
properties.source: Approximated from figure description: search moved 1,100 km NE of the previous area to a 319,000 km2 zone roughly 1,850 km west of Perth (Wikipedia narrative + ATSB final report Tables 13-14); polygon is a reconstructed box at that distance/size, no numeric corners published

properties.name: Southern Indian Ocean surface search, final northern areas (red/yellow/green zones along 7th arc, drifted areas C-F)
properties.operator: AMSA / ATSB / JACC (Australia), multinational; incl. ULB search by ADV Ocean Shield and Haixun 01
properties.outcome: Surface search ended 28 April 2014 with no debris found; acoustic detections (Haixun 01 at 25.975 S 101.461 E; Ocean Shield) later discounted
properties.source: Latitude extent backed by fetched values: ATSB report places the red/yellow/green priority areas at latitudes 20.2 S - 26 S along the 7th arc, 1,060-2,100 km west of Learmonth, spanning 217,000 km2; polygon is a computed-arc band 20.2-26 S with ~150 km half-width (encloses ~219,000 km2), shape approximated

properties.name: ULB acoustic search and Ocean Shield AUV follow-up (red zone, Zenith Plateau area)
properties.operator: ADV Ocean Shield (US Navy TPL-25 towed pinger locator + Phoenix International Artemis/Bluefin-21 AUV), HMS Echo, MV Haixun 01; RAAF AP-3C sonobuoys 6-17 Apr
properties.outcome: Four TPL detections 5-8 Apr 2014 (~33 kHz, near latitude 21 S) initially thought to be from the flight recorders' ULBs; AUV side-scan survey of 860 km2 (10 km radius around detection 2, 3 km around detections 1, 3, 4) completed 28 May 2014 found nothing, and the detections were later attributed to a faulty TPL test-ULB cable (Phoenix International lab tests). Haixun 01 detections (25.975 S 101.461 E, green zone) also discounted. Area eliminated
properties.source: Polygon corners are FETCHED values: red-zone corners U1-U4 from ATSB final report AE-2014-054 (3 Oct 2017) PDF p.194, appendix 'Identification of the most probable final location of flight MH370' (U1 20°16'02.71"S 104°17'04.86"E, U2 20°29'10.22"S 105°01'14.01"E, U4 22°14'21.49"S 104°08'34.81"E, U3 21°55'50.84"S 103°29'13.66"E); the full red/yellow/green ULB zone was 375 NM x 40 NM. Ocean Shield operated in this red zone (final report p.48); AUV ops 'up near latitude 21°S' (p.130). areaKm2 is the officially reported 860 km2 AUV sonar coverage; the red-zone polygon itself encloses ~16,200 km2. Narrative and dates from final report pp.45-51 and ATSB 'Definition of Underwater Search Areas' (18 Aug 2014) pp.11-15

properties.name: ATSB-led underwater search, 120,000 km2 wide area along the 7th arc
properties.operator: ATSB / JACC for Malaysia-Australia-China tripartite; contractors Fugro (Discovery, Equator, Supporter) and Phoenix International (GO Phoenix)
properties.outcome: Completed to high confidence without locating the aircraft; search suspended 17 January 2017. First Principles Review then identified a remaining 25,000 km2 area centred near 34 S 93 E, NE of this zone
properties.source: Latitude extent fetched from ATSB final report text (search area extended from 32.8 S, ultimately to 39.5 S along the 7th arc; wide area ~50 NM total width; ~120,000 km2 announced April 2015); polygon is a computed-7th-arc band 39.5 S to 32.5 S with 46.5 km half-width (encloses ~117,000 km2), shape approximated from report figures

properties.name: Ocean Infinity underwater search 2018 (Seabed Constructor), 7th-arc corridor extended north
properties.operator: Ocean Infinity (Seabed Constructor, 8 AUVs), 'no find, no fee' contract with Malaysia
properties.outcome: More than 112,000 km2 searched, starting in the CSIRO/First-Principles 25,000 km2 area (~35.6 S) and extending north along the arc to ~25 S; aircraft not found, points of interest classified geological; search ended June 2018
properties.source: Extent approximated from published descriptions (start in the 33-36 S remaining-priority area, progression north along the 7th arc to ~25 S; Ocean Infinity weekly updates give cumulative km2 only, no corners); polygon is a computed-7th-arc band 36.5 S to 25 S with 33 km half-width (encloses ~112,000 km2)

properties.name: Ocean Infinity underwater search 2025-26 (Armada fleet), 15,000 km2 refined zone
properties.operator: Ocean Infinity (Armada 78 06 in Feb-Apr 2025; Armada 86 05 from 30 Dec 2025), 'no find, no fee' agreement with Malaysian Ministry of Transport (~USD 70M on success)
properties.outcome: Concluded without a find: two phases (25-28 Mar 2025 and 31 Dec 2025 - 23 Jan 2026, vessel departed the search area 23 Jan 2026) surveyed 7,571 km2 of the ~15,000 km2 target zone over 28 operational days; 'none of the collected data identified the location of the wreckage' (Malaysian MOT). Agreement extended 1 Jul 2026 to 30 Jun 2027 with ~7,428 km2 remaining, operations paused for southern winter Nov 2026 - Apr 2027
properties.source: Position backed by fetched statements: OI/MOT describe a 15,000 km2 zone between latitudes 33 S and 36 S, wider off the 7th arc than previously searched, in the region of the UGIB LEP (-34.2342, 93.7875); polygon is a computed-7th-arc band 36 S to 33 S with half-width scaled to enclose ~15,000 km2 - the real zone is irregular (hotspot boxes extending beyond the previously searched corridor), so this is a strong approximation

properties.name: CAPTIO proposed new search zone (Blelly & Marchand, unsearched)
properties.operator: Patrick Blelly & Jean-Luc Marchand / CAPTIO (proposed, never executed)
properties.outcome: A proposal, not a conducted search. CAPTIO recommend a ~14 NM-wide trapezoid extending the 2018-searched corridor ~37 NM further south along their reconstructed 177 deg true track, anchored on their northernmost point of impact 35 deg 39' S 93 deg 01' E (a piloted controlled-ditching end-of-flight). Much of this 33-36 S band was subsequently covered by the failed 2025-26 Ocean Infinity search without a find; CAPTIO argue the specific southern extension was not fully scanned.
properties.source: Blelly & Marchand (CAPTIO v4a, May/June 2022), 'Analysis of Flight MH370', Executive Summary p.1 and section 5 'The proposed new search zone' p.17 (fetched 2026-07-17): '~14 Nm wide trapeze prolongating the already scanned zone to the south by ~37 Nm ... margin of ~7 Nm on each side of the true track of 177 deg ... this area of ~600 Nm2'; crash-zone endpoints from sections 2.8-2.9 (min -35.6650 S 93.0130 E, max -36.0269 S 93.0364 E). Polygon here is DIGITIZED from those two published crash-zone endpoints extended +/-7 NM half-width about the 177 deg track and +7 NM north/south contingency (encloses ~502 NM2 / ~1721 km2); areaKm2 carries CAPTIO's stated ~600 NM2 figure. https://www.mh370-caption.net/wp-content/uploads/Blelly-Analysis-of-Flight-MH370.pdf
